articles.title: Crude oil prices extend fall on smoother flows through Strait of Hormuz; Brent below $77 per barrel
articles.description: US-Iran peace talks: Global benchmark Brent crude futures for August delivery declined 0.45% to $76.73 per barrel on Wednesday after falling 1.1% in the previous session.

articles.title: Oil prices extend decline on expectations of smoother crude flows via Hormuz
articles.description: Oil prices dipped to four-month lows as tensions eased between the US and Iran, paving the way for more tankers to navigate the Strait of Hormuz. Following peace talks and a sanctions waiver, Iran's oil sales are expected to increase. Discussions are ongoing regarding navigation in the vital waterway, with ship-tracking data indicating a gradual resumption of traffic.

articles.title: From Gift Nifty, Nasdaq selloff, US dollar to oil prices: 10 key things that changed for Indian stock market overnight
articles.description: Gift Nifty was trading around 23,874 level, a premium of nearly 21 points from the Nifty futures’ previous close, indicating a muted start for the Indian stock market indices.

articles.title: GIFT Nifty signals positive start for Sensex, Nifty; Brent Crude near $76
articles.description: Global markets trade cautiously with tech sell-off continuing and crude oil prices under $80 supporting investors. GIFT Nifty signals a positive start for Indian markets amid mixed global cues on June 24, 2026.

articles.title: Oil extends slide on expectations of smoother crude flows via Hormuz
articles.description: Oil prices fell more than 1% on ​Wednesday, extending this week's losses and trading near four-month lows, on signs that more oil ‌tankers stranded in the Gulf are set to move out of the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: Global Market Today: Asia stocks rebound from tech selloff, Kospi jumps
articles.description: Asian markets saw a cautious rebound Wednesday after a significant tech-led global selloff, reigniting worries about the AI rally's sustainability. South Korea's Kospi surged, led by Samsung Electronics, following a steep decline. Investors now keenly await Micron Technology's results for clues on AI infrastructure demand, with some strategists predicting further downside for the semiconductor sector. Meanwhile, oil prices dipped as tanker traffic visibility improved.

articles.title: Why falling oil prices are bad news for packaged-food companies
articles.description: A memorandum of understanding between the U.S. and Iran, and a drop in oil prices, make it harder for food companies to justify price hikes.
